armed-conflicts.headline: US-Iran conflict escalates with reciprocal strikes in Middle East
armed-conflicts.summary: Tensions between the US and Iran escalated significantly on July 13, 2026, with the US launching new offensive strikes against Iranian military targets, and Iran retaliating with strikes on US military assets in several Gulf states. Both nations are asserting control over the critical Strait of Hormuz, threatening global shipping and prompting calls for de-escalation from the UN Secretary-General.

regions.name: Tehran, Iran
regions.detail: Unconfirmed reports of major US strikes on IRGC headquarters and an underground missile base, with allegations of IRGC Chief Ahmad Wahidi being eliminated.

regions.name: Strait of Hormuz
regions.detail: The US and Iran are asserting control over this vital waterway after Iranian attacks on commercial vessels and US retaliatory strikes.

regions.name: Prince Hassan Air Base, Jordan
regions.detail: Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed to have struck this US military base with missiles and drones.

regions.name: Sheikh Isa Air Base, Bahrain
regions.detail: Iran claimed a strike on this base, part of a second phase of retaliatory operations against US military targets.

regions.name: Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait
regions.detail: Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed to have struck this US military base as part of their retaliatory actions.

regions.name: Khuzestan province, Iran
regions.detail: A US strike targeting an agricultural water pumping station reportedly killed one person.

regions.name: Washington D.C., United States
regions.detail: The US Central Command reported completing a new wave of offensive strikes against Iran.

regions.name: Bandar Abbas, Iran
regions.detail: Explosions were reported as the US ended its latest strikes against Iran.

human-rights.headline: Rohingya face deadly landslides; digital rights and AI safety concerns rise
human-rights.summary: Rohingya refugees in Bangladesh are facing deadly landslides in overcrowded camps, worsened by funding shortages for vital infrastructure. Globally, the human rights landscape saw discussions around digital rights, with a new auditing technique for generative AI models developed to prevent illegal content generation. However, concerns remain regarding systemic racism in France, a restrictive Freedom of Information Bill in Malaysia, and police actions against activists in Nepal.

regions.name: Cox's Bazar, Bangladesh
regions.detail: Deadly landslides killed at least 17 and displaced over 3,000 Rohingya refugees in overcrowded camps, exacerbated by aid funding cuts.

regions.name: Paris, France
regions.detail: Human Rights Watch criticized France's new anti-racism plan for failing to address systemic racism and lacking disaggregated data collection.

regions.name: Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
regions.detail: A proposed Freedom of Information Bill was deemed deeply flawed and a betrayal of the right to know by civil society organizations.

regions.name: Kathmandu, Nepal
regions.detail: Police detained Gen Z activists protesting issues related to flood-affected squatters, raising concerns over shrinking space for dissent.

regions.name: Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States
regions.detail: MIT researchers developed a new auditing technique to test generative AI models for malicious capabilities without prompting illegal outputs, enhancing AI safety.

regions.name: Iran
regions.detail: The Baha'i minority continues to face fierce persecution amidst protests and war, with detainees held without formal charges.

climate-events.headline: US Upper Midwest and North Carolina face severe weather and flooding
climate-events.summary: A dangerous weather pattern is anticipated across the US Upper Midwest from July 13-29, with NOAA warning of heavy rainfall, flash flooding, and severe thunderstorms. Concurrently, parts of North Carolina experienced flash flood watches and warnings due to excessive rain, though some warnings later expired. In Bangladesh, deadly landslides in Rohingya refugee camps highlighted vulnerability to monsoon seasons exacerbated by deforestation.

regions.name: Upper Midwest, United States
regions.detail: NOAA issued a severe weather alert for July 13-29, predicting heavy rainfall, flash flooding, damaging winds, large hail, and frequent lightning.

regions.name: Western North Carolina, United States
regions.detail: Flash flood watches and warnings were in effect for several counties due to excessive rain.

regions.name: Harnett County, North Carolina, United States
regions.detail: A flash flood warning was issued, though heavy rain had ended and flooding was no longer expected to pose a threat.

regions.name: Cox's Bazar, Bangladesh
regions.detail: Deadly landslides affecting Rohingya refugee camps during the monsoon season, linked to deforested hillsides.

public-health.headline: Measles case in LA; Ebola crisis in DRC; Sudan cholera outbreak worsens
public-health.summary: A confirmed measles case in a traveler led to public exposure warnings in Los Angeles County, marking the seventh case in 2026. The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo, caused by a lethal Bundibugyo strain, remains a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. In Sudan, drone attacks have intensified a humanitarian crisis, leading to the spread of cholera and severe water shortages in the Kordofan region.

regions.name: Los Angeles, California, United States
regions.detail: The Public Health Department is investigating a confirmed measles case in a traveler with possible exposures at LAX and other public locations.

regions.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
regions.detail: An Ebola outbreak, specifically the lethal Bundibugyo strain, has been declared a Public Health Emergency of International Concern.

regions.name: Kordofan, Sudan
regions.detail: Drone strikes are exacerbating a humanitarian crisis, contributing to the spread of cholera and critically low water supplies.

regions.name: Paris, France
regions.detail: A new global report outlining strategies to improve hematologic care for women, addressing inequities in diagnosis and treatment, was presented at a congress.

regions.name: New York City, New York, United States
regions.detail: NYC Health held a virtual town hall for updates on a Legionnaires' Disease cluster investigation.

economic-crises.headline: Global markets volatile as US-Iran tensions disrupt oil; US inflation data awaited
economic-crises.summary: Global markets experienced volatility as escalating US-Iran tensions in the Strait of Hormuz caused crude oil prices to jump and Indian equity benchmarks to open lower. Investors are closely monitoring the situation in the Middle East due to potential disruptions to global energy supplies. Attention is also on upcoming US inflation reports and major bank earnings this week, which are expected to influence market stability.

regions.name: Strait of Hormuz
regions.detail: Geopolitical tensions between the US and Iran are causing crude oil prices to surge and threatening global energy supplies.

regions.name: Mumbai, India
regions.detail: Indian equity benchmarks, Sensex and Nifty, opened lower due to renewed US-Iran tensions.

regions.name: New York, United States
regions.detail: US stock futures slipped as investors awaited inflation data and major corporate earnings reports.

regions.name: Tokyo, Japan
regions.detail: Japan's Nikkei 225 and Topix indices traded mostly higher despite heightened geopolitical risks.

regions.name: Seoul, South Korea
regions.detail: South Korea's Kospi advanced, but SK Hynix saw heavy selling after its US listing.

democratic-erosion.headline: Report highlights global democratic erosion; US Senate faces uncertain agenda
democratic-erosion.summary: A Lowy Institute report released today detailed how democracies erode from within, citing cases in Hungary, Mexico, the United States, South Africa, and India. The US Senate faces an uncertain agenda following the sudden death of Senator Lindsey Graham and the ongoing absence of Senator Mitch McConnell, potentially impacting legislative priorities. Concerns also persist about democratic backsliding in the United States, with the V-Dem Institute classifying it as an 'electoral democracy' in 2026.

regions.name: Washington D.C., United States
regions.detail: The US Senate faces an uncertain agenda after the death of Senator Lindsey Graham; democratic backsliding is also a concern.

regions.name: Budapest, Hungary
regions.detail: Constitutional power grab cited as a case study in how democracies erode from within.

regions.name: Mexico City, Mexico
regions.detail: Cartel-weakened institutions were highlighted as an example of democratic erosion.

regions.name: Pretoria, South Africa
regions.detail: Democratic disillusionment was noted as a factor in democratic erosion.

regions.name: New Delhi, India
regions.detail: Communal violence was cited as a contributing factor to democratic erosion.

regions.name: Kigali, Rwanda
regions.detail: A training course on Democratic Backsliding and Authoritarian Resurgence is scheduled to take place.

regions.name: Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
regions.detail: A new Freedom of Information Bill is being criticized by civil society for undermining the right to information.

humanitarian.headline: Deadly landslides hit Rohingya camps; Sudan crisis worsens; aid to Venezuela
humanitarian.summary: Deadly landslides in Rohingya refugee camps in Cox's Bazar, Bangladesh, killed at least 17 people and displaced over 3,000, with aid funding cuts exacerbating the crisis. In Sudan's Kordofan region, continued drone attacks are worsening the humanitarian situation, spreading cholera and leading to critically low water supplies. Following devastating earthquakes, Venezuela received significant humanitarian aid, including an emergency grant from Japan and 30 tons of aid from Türkiye.

regions.name: Cox's Bazar, Bangladesh
regions.detail: Deadly landslides claimed lives and displaced thousands of Rohingya refugees, with calls for urgent aid and reduced overcrowding.

regions.name: Kordofan, Sudan
regions.detail: The humanitarian crisis is deepening due to ongoing drone strikes, leading to cholera outbreaks and severe water shortages.

regions.name: Caraballeda, Venezuela
regions.detail: Humanitarian aid, including tents and medical supplies from Türkiye, arrived in Venezuela following devastating twin earthquakes.

regions.name: Simon Bolivar International Airport, Venezuela
regions.detail: Received 30 tons of humanitarian aid from Türkiye following devastating earthquakes.

regions.name: Sub-Saharan Africa
regions.detail: Fertilizer shipments from the Middle East are disrupted by the US-Iran conflict, potentially accelerating food insecurity.

regions.name: Tokyo, Japan
regions.detail: The Government of Japan provided a $3.5 million emergency grant for humanitarian assistance in Venezuela.

regions.name: Ankara, Türkiye
regions.detail: Türkiye delivered 30 tons of humanitarian aid to earthquake-hit Venezuela.

regions.name: United Kingdom
regions.detail: Over 16,000 refugees are unable to reunite with their families due to the suspension of family reunion routes.
